issueData.title: Prototype Pollution
issueData.description: lodash before 4.17.20 is vulnerable to Prototype Pollution via the merge, mergeWith, and defaultsDeep functions. An attacker can supply a payload such as '{"constructor": {"prototype": {"admin": true}}}' to pollute the Object prototype, potentially bypassing security controls or enabling Remote Code Execution when prototype properties are later accessed in a privileged context.

issueData.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
issueData.description: axios before 1.6.0 is vulnerable to Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF). An attacker can bypass the proxy configuration and send requests to arbitrary hosts by setting a crafted hostname in the URL. Upgrading from 0.21.x to 1.6.x is a MAJOR version jump with BREAKING CHANGES: default headers API changed, transformRequest/transformResponse signatures changed, and CancelToken is removed in favour of AbortController.

issueData.title: Prototype Pollution
issueData.description: minimist before 1.2.6 is vulnerable to Prototype Pollution. The library is used transitively by mkdirp (0.5.x) which is a dependency of this project. An attacker can inject properties onto Object.prototype via the argument parsing functions. This is a TRANSITIVE vulnerability — minimist is not directly imported but reached through mkdirp → minimist.

issueData.title: Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS)
issueData.description: semver before 7.5.2 is vulnerable to Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) via the semver.clean() function. An attacker can cause a denial of service by passing a specially crafted version string. This is a low-severity issue with no code changes required — a manifest-only bump is sufficient.
